stemHtml: According to the International Telecommunication Union (ITU) code, a radio signal may be classified by three symbols. The abbreviation A3E is used for the type of transmission in which the carrier wave is:

options.a: A continuous wave interrupted with a keyed Morse code ident as used by an NDB.
options.b: Amplitude modulated to transmit the Morse code ident, as is used by a VOR.
options.c: Amplitude modulated by a speech signal, such as is used for VHF-COM.
options.d: Interrupted to transmit the Morse code identification, as used by an NDB.
